title: abies rubra
intro: Abies rubra, also known as Red Fir, is a species of evergreen coniferous tree native to the western United States and Canada. The resin of the Abies rubra plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of Abies rubra have also been used to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Abies rubra can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
